title: crotalaria scariosa
intro: Crotalaria scariosa, also known as the "rattle box" or "rattle bush" due to the noise made by its seed pods, is a flowering plant native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and seeds of Crotalaria scariosa have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and have also been used to boost the immune system. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for modern use.
